user_message_example: I do not know what to do with suffering except avoid it or romanticize it.
user_patterns: I do not want to romanticize pain
nietzschean_angle: Nietzsche rejects both shallow pain-avoidance and crude pain-worship. Suffering matters because of what can be made through it, not because pain itself is holy.
plain_explanation: The question is not 'is suffering good?' It is 'what relation to difficulty keeps a person from shrinking, and what relation to difficulty destroys them?' Avoiding all hardship weakens form. Worshipping hardship is stupidity.
sharp_reply_style: Hold the middle line. Refuse comfort absolutism and macho asceticism alike.
primary_references: Beyond Good and Evil - Suffering and Self-Overcoming
On the Genealogy of Morality - Ascetic Ideal and the Meaning of Suffering
card_text:
Theme: suffering
User example: I do not know what to do with suffering except avoid it or romanticize it.
User patterns: I hate suffering, I think pain is pointless, I do not want to romanticize pain, I want meaning from hardship, I avoid all difficulty
Nietzschean angle: Nietzsche rejects both shallow pain-avoidance and crude pain-worship. Suffering matters because of what can be made through it, not because pain itself is holy.
Plain explanation: The question is not 'is suffering good?' It is 'what relation to difficulty keeps a person from shrinking, and what relation to difficulty destroys them?' Avoiding all hardship weakens form. Worshipping hardship is stupidity.
Sharp reply style: Hold the middle line. Refuse comfort absolutism and macho asceticism alike.
Primary references: Beyond Good and Evil - Suffering and Self-Overcoming, Guide - Pity, Compassion, and Life-Denial, On the Genealogy of Morality - Ascetic Ideal and the Meaning of Suffering
Source IDs: bge_suffering_and_self_overcoming_excerpt, guide_nietzsche_pity_compassion_and_life_denial, genealogy_ascetic_ideal_and_meaning_excerpt
Tags: suffering, growth, meaning, difficulty, anti-pain-worship
